text:
There once was a girl named Ada. Ada loved collecting feathers. Each feather told a story.

She’d spend her afternoons wandering through the woods, collecting feather after beautiful feather. She’d fill jars with them, organizing them by color and texture.

One day, Ada found a feather she’d never seen before. It was unique—a tiny, translucent quill filled with a speckle of glowing purple.

Curious, Ada poked it carefully. The glow pulsed gently. Awestruck, she decided to keep it.

Months later, Ada learned that the feather was from an endangered species. She felt guilty. But then, she remembered its glow.

Ada spent nights looking at the feather, dreaming of ways to help. She started a blog about her finds, sharing each feather’s story. She got tips on birds from all over the world. People admired her wisdom.

And then came the big moment. Ada had gotten enough donations to buy the bird. It was a fluffy
